note: Cycle 78 retro/grooming (2026-07-11): statuses normalized to enum; ~150 shipped/superseded items archived to backlog-archive.json. This file = LIVE queue only. Terminal record + all ship SHAs/PRs/TestFlight versions preserved in backlog-archive.json (see resolution fields).

items.title: OWNER: named-course-from-elsewhere bug (Marine Park from Pittsburgh) + map-based course search
items.why: OWNER 2026-07-11: (A) BUG — named 'Marine Park' (NY) but searched GPS-nearby Pittsburgh; parser only hears on-screen GPS-list courses, drops the name, dispatches anyway. (B) FEATURE — map-based course search (DB bbox + cached OSM markers, golf-only, budget-aware) vs the bland checklist. Plan specs/course-selection-ux-plan.md. Slices A0 (stop-the-lie: extract unresolved name + gate dispatch + honest ack) → A1 (backend selector-centered discovery) → A2 (voice resolution via unified search) → A3 (clarify turn) → B1 (/api/courses/in-bounds) → B2 (map mode) → B3 (polish). BUG A0 first.
items.a0_status: A0 (stop-the-lie) DONE 2026-07-11 @ea6e6d3 — unresolvedCourseNames extraction (conservative at/on/over-at/out-at capture, prefs-vocabulary-bounded, distinctive-word required) + dispatch gating (dispatched=false when a named course is unresolved OR a listed-name miss exists) + honest ack naming the course; LLM-pass dropped names mapped in; Marine-Park-from-Pittsburgh fixtures RED->GREEN + corpus seed:teetime:010. Reviewer SHIP. Remaining: A1 backend selector-centered discovery, A2 voice resolution via unified search, A3 clarify turn, B1-B3 map search.
items.a1_status: A1 (backend selector-centered discovery) DONE 2026-07-11 @e212621 — when resolved selectors carry centers (selection.py), discover OSM around each selector center (~5km via SELECTOR_DISCOVERY_RADIUS_MILES=3.1 + _radius_meters 5km floor) and additively merge with GPS-origin discovery; distance stays HONEST from the golfer's GPS origin (no fake-nearby); distance>max_distance prune skipped for selector-matched courses (un-selected still pruned); pure near-me is byte-identical (no selectors -> no extra finder call/dedupe; within-radius or already-discovered selector -> skipped). 3 new tests incl. RED-before Marine-Park-from-Pittsburgh (~319mi honest distance, finder invoked around Brooklyn). Reviewer SHIP (all 7 properties, RED-before verified by hand); QA ruff+69 DB-free pass, CI Backend gate SUCCESS. Backend-only, zero frontend. Non-blocking follow-up: per-selector OSM fan-out uncapped (naturally bounded to DB-resolved scattered ids). Remaining: A2 voice resolution (resolve spoken name -> course; the slice that completes the fix), A3 clarify turn, B1-B3 map search.

items.title: OWNER BUG P0: caddie grounds on mock 178, not GPS/selected-tee (Bethpage hole 3) — argues wrong yardage
items.resolution: Cycle 90 (2026-07-11), all 4 slices landed on integration/next (PR #133) @275dee3/de9a12f/b9c6f29/2eb7dea + reviewer fold @007fa0a + designer fold @6591d9b. Shared pure resolveHoleYardage (GPS-to-green 5-800y gate -> selected-tee-card -> tee-geom [par-3 exact / par-4-5 floor] -> real-card -> honest null); mock 178 (HoleIllustration.tsx) BANNED from every caddie/grounding surface (grep-verified, kept only for the unmapped paper illustration). Selected-tee yards captured at setup + hydrated from mapped course; tee-anchor untagged-box ordinal/back-most rule -> Bethpage hole 3 resolves Black box (231/232). GPS + provenance-labeled yardage plumbed to caddie grounding (additive distance_to_green_yards/hole_yards/yardage_basis request fields; fake yards=400 default removed; shared _format_yardage_line provenance formatter, par-4/5 tee-geom stated as 'at least' floor). YARDAGE_GROUNDING_RULE (text+realtime): GPS/selected-tee is ground truth, caddie ADOPTS the player's stated number, never 'on the card' unless it's their tee card. All surfaces (header, tiles, plays-like, offline card, caddie voice) agree on number + honesty. RED-before Bethpage-hole-3 fixture (legacy->ordinal, 231 not 178) now GREEN. Reviewer SHIP, designer SHIP; gates green (lint/tsc/voice 278/vitest 2100/build; ruff + pytest 2067; PR CI Frontend+Backend+E2E SUCCESS). Rides bundle #133; merges with owner's next 'ship it'.
items.why: OWNER 2026-07-11 (screenshots): caddie says '178 on the card' (a MOCK ILLUSTRATION constant, HoleIllustration.tsx:20) but owner plays 231 from Black tees; GPS active but unused; caddie ARGUES. Plan specs/caddie-yardage-gps-selected-tee-plan.md. FIX (4 slices): (1) shared resolveHoleYardage (GPS-to-green→selected-tee-card→tee-geom→real-card→null), ban mock from caddie surfaces; (2) capture/hydrate selected-tee yards + tee-anchor untagged-ordinal/back-most; (3) plumb GPS+basis to caddie grounding (+ request fields, prompt provenance formatter); (4) YARDAGE_GROUNDING_RULE so caddie adopts player's number, never 'on the card'. Bethpage-hole-3 231-vs-178 fixture RED today. Core caddie-trust bug.

items.title: Show + wire the caddie orb on course-detail pages (close the omnipresence gap)
items.why: Cycle-84 orb-wiring audit latent note: /courses/[id] hid the orb but specs/omnipresent-caddie-orb-plan.md §1 lists /courses(/*) as SHOW and the owner directive is 'omnipresent... everywhere it belongs.' Simply showing it would hit the legacy-courses-floor guard (dead mic), so this needed BOTH: show it AND scope that guard to the list page. General converse is the honest default on a detail page (no bespoke task context this slice).
items.resolution: shipped-to-bundle (integration/next, PR #133). NOTICEABLE — orb now visible + usable on /courses/[id]. Cycle 85 (2026-07-11). Closes the ONLY real gap the cycle-84 orb-wiring audit found (detail pages hid the orb; plan §1 lists /courses(/*) as SHOW). FIX (Option B — scope via the context SIGNAL, no pathname coupling in the host): looperContextForPath (looper-bus.ts) scopes context 'courses' to the LIST route only (/courses, trailing-slash tolerant); /courses/[id] → 'general', so the host's legacy-courses-floor guard (CaddieOrbSheet.tsx:127, unchanged code — comment only) now only ever fires on the list page and a detail-page orb falls through to the general converse sheet (ask the caddie anything incl. about this course) — NOT a dead mic. shouldShowCaddieOrb adds /courses/ to SHOW_PREFIXES. Designer BLOCKER folded: bumped CourseDetailClient.tsx bottom padding 32px→88px (the established /players + /profile orb-clearance pattern) so the fixed orb clears the in-flow 'Start a round here' CTA (screenshot-verified no overlap). NO bespoke course-detail task context this cycle (deferred follow-up if warranted). Commits @5a29ebb (feature) + @f0c0ae4 (clearance). Reviewer: SHIP (correct, in-scope, 48/48 tests, dead-mic regression test proven meaningful, no double-mic, no weakened tests). Designer: PASS after the padding fix. Gates green on head: lint clean, tsc 0, voice 277/277, vitest 2055/2055, build ok. Backend untouched. SILENT — no ship/ping; rides bundle #133.

items.title: Orb S5: orb state motion/haptics tuning + telemetry + inset audit + copy
items.why: Orb epic S5 (§6). Orb idle/listening/thinking/confirming motion+haptics tuning (framer-motion T.springSoft, lib/haptics — no new motion language); voiceEvent telemetry on orb summon/task-dispatch/fall-through (measures discoverability risk); per-page bottom-inset audit (orb vs page CTAs); copy pass. Designer.
items.resting_depth_glow_status: DONE (c05e706, cycle 93, 2026-07-11) — owner S5 feel-feedback ('orb is a little boring visually... add some depth or some kind of glow'). Designer-led, live-verified: CaddieOrb resting style now a radial-gradient ink dome (circle at 32% 26%, T.inkSoft→T.ink 62%) + refined inset emboss (paper-toned top rim + bottom inner shadow) + a low-alpha paper-toned ambient halo (0 0 16px rgba(244,241,234,0.12), 0 spread), all ONE static boxShadow stack — NO pulse (a breathing glow reads as SaaS 'AI thinking'; NORTHSTAR-out). Auto-adapts to surface (near-invisible on paper, soft aura over dark map) so no theme-conditional code. Resting-only; pointer/state/placement/size/mark untouched. Reviewer SHIP, designer SIGN-OFF (raised ink-medallion/wax-seal read, calm not neon). Halo-over-dark check deferred to device/TestFlight. NOTICEABLE, rides bundle #133. Remaining S5: motion/haptics tuning, telemetry, per-page inset audit, copy pass.

items.title: EPIC: omnipresent context-aware caddie orb (THE CRUX) — replaces center-nav orb
items.why: OWNER 2026-07-10 (verbatim: 'That is the CRUX of this app... highest priority'): kill the center-nav orb; ONE omnipresent floating orb across all relevant pages, SAME location everywhere, CADDIE icon (not mic). Round-page floating pill STAYS. Orb is CONTEXT-AWARE per page: tee-time → listens days/where/who and DISPATCHES the search; start round → voice round setup; start tournament → voice tournament setup (the stranded parse layer from the audit gets wired HERE); My Card → stats Q&A / what-to-work-on coaching. Fable plan first (specs/omnipresent-caddie-orb-plan.md), then sequenced slices. Supersedes floating-island-tab-nav placement; keeps its one-invocation rule.

items.title: Tournament live tracking (multi-phone standings without reload)
items.why: AUDIT: NO live tracking — useEffect([id]) loads once; REST+localStorage only; two phones don't see each other's scores without reload. Largest lift: polling or SSE so standings update live. Backend + staging-testable → owner-ping-worthy when ready.

items.title: Per-round format + course at tournament setup (schema change)
items.why: AUDIT: setup collects only a round COUNT; Tournament schema (models.py:182, db/models.py:263) has no per-round course/format fields. Extend setup to configure N rounds each with course+format; add schema fields (+ Alembic migration — guarded). Backend + frontend.

items.title: Handicap-aware NET leaderboard mode
items.why: AUDIT: computeStandings (TournamentPageClient.tsx:96-134) is GROSS strokes only — no handicaps, no games/format factored. Add a Net mode (alongside Gross/To-Par) using player handicaps. User-facing.

items.title: Tournament leaderboard motion + haptics pass (owner asked)
items.why: AUDIT: ZERO framer-motion/haptics in all 3 tournament screens (owner explicitly asked where to add). Opportunities: FLIP layout re-sort of leaderboard rows on standings change (haptic light on moved row); leader crossfade+haptic success on new leader; overtake underline sweep+haptic medium; tab-bar layoutId pill slide+selection haptic; round active→final stamp; settlement reveal stagger. Designer pass (NORTHSTAR calm — motion serves clarity, not flash). Files: TournamentPageClient.tsx.

items.title: Fetch real tee-time availability for EVERY course (API → scrape → AI-call ladder)
items.why: PLAN: specs/teetime-availability-everywhere-plan.md (Fable). KEY FINDING: Marine Park was NEVER phone-only — its site links a live EZLinks online-booking portal; the dead-end was a capability-DETECTION gap (we only know foreUP), not missing data. Ladder = 4 rungs: API → widget-XHR replay → headless render → AI-call → honest-empty. Sequenced S4a-S4f below. Most of the epic is engine-adapter breadth + per-course capability detection, NOT exotic scraping. OWNER DIRECTIVE 2026-07-10 (screenshot, Marine Park 'No online booking — call the pro shop'): showing a bare phone number is NOT good enough. Every course MUST return real availability in SOME form. Ladder: (1) clean booking-engine API where one exists (foreUP live; EVALUATE adding Chronogolf/Lightspeed/GolfNow-EZLinks/TeeItUp APIs), (2) WEB SCRAPE the rendered availability widget where no clean JSON API exists, (3) AI phone-call the pro shop (PR #124 caller) as the final fallback for phone-only courses. Owner explicitly OK with building a scraper if it's the best per-course option. PLAN FIRST on Fable (per fable-for-plan-agents): per-engine option eval, scraping feasibility+ToS/legal+infra (headless browser for JS-rendered widgets), caching (GolfAPI-style budget discipline), integration with the reachability router.

items.title: Spike: cascaded STT (Deepgram nova-3 + keyterms + confidence) feeding the live caddie as text
items.why: SPIKE DONE (PR #126, FEASIBLE conditional-go, hybrid Option C recommended). BLOCKED on the go/no-go latency read: prod has ~0 usable eos_to_first_audio data (iOS telemetry drop). Unblock via voicetel-timing-immediate-flush, then read caddie-turn vs caddie-rt p90; <1.5-2s delta -> build hybrid, 3s+ -> stay put (vocab-bias+input-grounding already shipped may be enough). Confidence-gate code ready behind a flag in #126. OWNER 2026-07-09 (reliability): the SOTA reliability architecture — route the live transcript through our own Deepgram nova-3 (already used for the sheet WITH keyterms), gate on confidence, THEN the caddie responds to TEXT ('treat speech as rendering not reasoning' — our own agent-architecture study). Garbled input caught BEFORE a reply. Trades some latency for reliability+observability vs the current speech-to-speech. Spike: measure the latency delta + reliability gain on real outdoor audio. specs/voice-transcription-reliability-research.md. Fable plan.

items.title: Spike: target-speaker filtering (voice enrollment + on-device TSVAD)
items.why: SPIKE DONE (PR #127): Tier-1 mic constraints (echoCancel/noiseSuppress/autoGain + backend near_field) ALREADY SHIPPED (~70% of the value, no code win left). Tier-2 TSVAD = FEASIBLE/medium as a NATIVE Core ML Capacitor plugin (WKWebView can't do threaded WASM); Personal-VAD arch, named pretrained models; pure tested SpeakerGate core committed unwired. GO when prioritized (sequence after voice-reliability). Tier-3 VoiceFilter = defer. OWNER 2026-07-09 (hands-free + only-owner's-voice): the real answer is a one-time voice ENROLLMENT (~5-10s -> speaker embedding) + a Target-Speaker VAD gate that opens the mic-to-model only when the enrolled owner speaks — rejects partners without breaking hands-free. Spike: which on-device (Core ML) TSVAD/speaker-embedding model, latency budget vs the realtime speech-to-speech path (preprocessing fights sub-second latency — on-device is the sweet spot), enrollment UX. NOT train-from-scratch (pretrained models exist). Full VoiceFilter extraction only if TSVAD gating insufficient. Fable plan. specs/voice-target-speaker-research.md.

items.title: Quiet clamp so header pill and Center tile never show two slightly-different numbers in the reconciled state
items.note: Designer watch-item from multi-tee (c682f7f): in the NORMAL non-card-only state, header prefers cardYards exactly while the Center tile prefers anchored geometry center — a golfer can still see e.g. 178y header next to a 181 tile. Honest (real GPS vs scorecard differ a couple yards) and the 'from the tee' caption explains it, but two stacked near-equal numbers is the pattern the owner flagged. Add a '<=X yd, don't render both' clamp or a quiet designer note. Not a bug; polish.
items.why: SCOPED 2026-07-10 — NOT a clean bug, a DESIGN QUESTION (do not autonomously build). Header shows cardYards (scorecard hole length from the selected tee, RoundPageClient ~1194); Center tile shows fcbFromTee.center (straight-line tee→green geometry). On a from-tee non-live hole these differ by design — on a dogleg the card follows the fairway while geometry is straight-line, so a small 'drift' is EXPECTED and arguably correct. A 'clamp' that snaps the tile to the card would HIDE the real straight-line-to-green number (useful info). Needs owner/designer call: (a) is the drift actually confusing on-device (validate on a real TestFlight screenshot — the multi-tee anchor fix already resolved the phantom 178/232 case), (b) if so, prefer a caption/label clarification over clamping the number. Deferred pending that decision.

items.title: Tee-time: file GolfNow + Supreme Golf + foreUP vendor applications
items.why: OWNER-ACTION (parallel, free): apply to GolfNow affiliate (affiliate.gnsvc.com / business-partnership form), Supreme Golf affiliate, and foreUP's vendor API program. Any approval upgrades Route 1 from scraping to sanctioned API. Blocked on the owner submitting the forms (needs a business identity/positioning) — the agent can draft the applications but the owner sends them.

items.title: Bethpage 7/11 guide-validation conflict: DIAGNOSED — carry-tolerance vs bunker footprint (decision needed)
items.why: INVESTIGATION 2026-07-09 (cycle 39, OFFLINE from committed fixture tests/fixtures/bethpage_overpass.json — same data prod ingested; no prod SSM needed). Holes 7 & 11 were persistently honest-empty (research ~4x each fails validate_guide). ROOT CAUSE = (c) validator over-strict, NOT (a) geometry or (b) OSM. EVIDENCE: (a) ruled out — extract_hole_hazards gives correct sides/carries matching reality: hole 7 par-5 dogleg-right (polyline 346->36->55deg) bunkers R 170/430/520 + L 355/525; hole 11 par-4 bunkers both sides L 245/415 R 270/325/420. All normal/correct non-numbered guide phrasings PASS the validator. (b) ruled out on-hole — 7/11 have ONLY bunkers, nearest water 395-431y OFF the played line (not in play); a water/OB mention is therefore a CORRECT rejection (OSM has no 'ob' polygon type so any OB phrase always rejects — expected). (c) CONFIRMED + reproduced: _side_and_carry_supported() checks a claimed carry vs each bunker's CENTROID +-25y, but Bethpage bunkers are large — hole 7's right cross-bunker centroid=170y actually SPANS 103-280y (178y wide). A web-researched guide naturally citing any carry in the real footprint (220/240/250/260) but >25y from centroid -> WHOLE guide REJECTED (demonstrated: all four reject). That is why grounded, reasonable numbered advice keeps failing = the persistence pattern. DECISION NEEDED (genuine product/risk call, not an obvious fix): (c1) widen the carry check to the bunker's surveyed near->far carry SPAN (recovers guides on big-bunker holes) — but this loosens the grounding/anti-laundering control that had a bypass closed just days ago (5e4b861 carry-tie-break laundering), so it REQUIRES the full adversarial treatment: Fable plan + eval-harness teeth (each new accept-case proven RED pre-fix) + adversarial review proving no false number laundering re-opens; vs (c2) accept status quo — honest-empty is SAFE and correct (caddie falls back to the grounded generic hazard line); large-bunker holes simply get no cached prose guide. Recommend (c1) as a properly-reviewed future build item IF cached guides on such holes are wanted; otherwise close as working-as-designed. Optional confirmation: an on-box research pass capturing the exact rejected text would pin which number/phrase fired in prod, but the local reproduction already demonstrates the mechanism — not worth an unattended prod SSM action. FILES: backend/app/caddie/guide_writer.py (_side_and_carry_supported, _has_side_flip, _CARRY_TOLERANCE_YARDS=25); backend/app/caddie/hazards.py (extract_hole_hazards, centroid-based carry). FORBIDDEN to inline-fix per tasks/lessons.md (geometry/validation change without full adversarial treatment).
items.resolution: diagnosed — decision needed (do NOT inline-fix: validator is the anti-injection/grounding control)

items.title: PLAYS/Center 'line vs card' dogleg hint (HELD — designer leans no)
items.why: Plan §4.5 (line-vs-card hint) built as a pure tested helper (lineVsCardHint in fcb-labels.ts) but intentionally NOT rendered. Designer recommendation (2026-07-08): DON'T add it as spec'd — the card already carries 7 microlabels and a second explanatory tag tips it toward annotated-dashboard (NORTHSTAR risk). If ever revisited, the comparison base MUST change: compare fcb.center to hole.yards (the real scorecard number), NOT the derived `distance` display placeholder (RoundPageClient.tsx:1058 = Math.max(80, hole.yards - round(hole.yards*0.6))), or the 'line' tag misfires on nearly every hole (fake-signal risk). Keep held pending owner ask.

items.title: Faster nearby search + GolfAPI universe as a verify layer
items.why: OWNER 2026-07-09: (a) nearby search takes a long time — reduce results shown and/or make the geo search more efficient (geo-indexed query, cached area results, smaller page); (b) use the GolfAPI to fetch the course universe ONCE ('I believe this is just one API call — do not spend a lot of calls') and use it as a VERIFY step so junk rows like 'Golf Course' or 'Pebble Beach 9th Hole' never surface. CONSTRAINTS: the GolfAPI key currently 401s (owner must fix the account first — BLOCKED for the universe half until then); the plan MUST verify the exact call count for a universe dump from GolfAPI docs BEFORE any call is spent (pagination risk; hard budget cap 45/mo); store the universe permanently (one-time cost); verify layer = fuzzy-match Places/OSM results against the canonical list, downrank non-matches (never hard-drop mapped/known-good courses). The latency half (a) is UNBLOCKED — build now.
items.resolution: partly-shipped — LATENCY half done-shipped-main (#114 5d20ec7, two-phase progressive nearby + geo-cell cache, TestFlight v1.0.888); UNIVERSE/verify half BLOCKED on the GolfAPI 401 key fix (owner-action) + docs-verified call count before spend

items.title: Glasses spike: triggered-clip shot detection + phone-free caddie (Meta DAT)
items.why: OWNER TABLED 2026-07-09 (revisit on his word; research preserved in specs/glasses-shot-tracking-research.md). OWNER 2026-07-09 thesis, researched (specs/glasses-shot-tracking-research.md): continuous-vision tracking INFEASIBLE (60-90min battery, 1-min clip caps, thermal shutoffs) but TRIGGERED-CLIP design is plausible — IMU/impact-audio trigger -> 5-10s 720p clip via Meta Wearables DAT (dev preview; 12MP cam + 5-mic + speakers accessible to OUR phone app) -> on-phone swing/ball classification, fused with GPS displacement. ~5-7 min total camera/round = inside battery. Replaces vocal confirmation with VISUAL — the owner's objection to phone-only sensing dissolves. Putts = first sensor with a real chance, but trigger reliability unproven. SHIPPING gated on Meta opening publishing (their 2026 date); PROTOTYPING possible now. STEP 0 ($0, today): owner pairs Ray-Ban Metas as BT audio with the live caddie — phone-free conversation works without any code. Spike = diagnostic-only, measure trigger precision/recall + battery on owner rounds before any UX.

items.title: Ask Caddie runs on the Realtime engine — hands-free like round setup
items.why: Owner 2026-07-08 (explicit direction, twice): 'the caddie still requires me to click before and after I'm talking instead of following the start round model where it detects for me.' Root: the sheet's Deepgram live path keeps failing on his iPhone -> fallback recorder has no VAD -> manual taps. DIRECTION: route the in-round Ask Caddie conversation through the OpenAI Realtime session (server VAD, same as setup; a warm session already exists for the round page orb). Sheet becomes the live transcript/answer view; the auto opening reco becomes the first spoken turn (compose with caddie-opening-reco-from-tee); barge-in native. NEEDS A FULL OPUS PLAN: session/tool grounding parity (hazards, memory, profile via session context), warm-path invariants (pinning tests untouchable), cost per round, fallback to the sheet path when Realtime can't connect, exit/mute affordances. Supersedes further Deepgram-sheet conversational patches.

slices.A: DONE (34c1222): Backend grounding parity for the Realtime mint (conversation history, green slope, last rec, recent shots) — guarded, byte-identical when absent; improves the live orb today. Reviewer CLEAN + QA PASS.
slices.B: Live-mode transcript-view shell in CaddieSheet behind caddie-live-mode flag (default off), mocked client in tests. Additive UI.
slices.C: C1 SHIPPED (#110, ac9bec0, TestFlight v1.0.840): flag-gated (?liveMode=1, default OFF) Realtime transport in the Ask Caddie sheet — continuous listen + opening turn via sendText + mute/close + honest pre-connect fallback. Double-reviewed. Owner on-device verification pending -> drives flag default flip (Slice E).
slices.D: DONE (f303c91, on integration/next bundle PR #111; SILENT, flag default OFF): live-session resilience — post-connected drop -> ONE quiet cold-mint reconnect (transcript preserved, no re-greet); reconnect fail -> classic tap-to-talk fallback in-place with conversation seeded (never a dead sheet); in-flight start() resurrection guard; bounded 1/activation; idle-vs-drop classification; cross-client transcript ordering. Opus plan specs/caddie-realtime-slice-d-plan.md. Gates green (voice 274/274, vitest 1686, pinning 104/104 unmodified); reviewer CLEAN.
slices.E: Cost/idle polish, telemetry, flag default flip after owner device sign-off.
slices.A2: CONDITIONAL/LEAN interim: sentence-level TTS pipelining on the CLASSIC sheet path (synth+play sentence 1 while rest streams) — cuts ~1-2s off first-voice. BUILD only if Slice C wont land device-verified within ~2 cycles; durable (classic path is the permanent fallback), not throwaway. SKIP if C imminent. See plan 6.5.4.
slices.telemetry: Stage-timing voicetel events (eos_to_transcript, transcript_to_first_token, first_token_to_first_audio, eos_to_first_audio headline) so the budget is visible on-device. Headline event must flush immediately (iOS voicetel flush-drop). See plan 6.5.3.

items.classification: noticeable (Slice C is the owner-visible hands-free change; A/B are enablers, A improves the live caddie today)
items.top_metric: END-TO-END LATENCY: <=~1.5-2.0s from end-of-speech to caddie VOICE start (owner 2026-07-07: long pause between speaking, transcript, text, then voice). See plan 6.5.
items.resolution: in-progress (Slices A+A2+telemetry shipped; C1 SHIPPED #110 flag-gated live mode v1.0.840; D landed on bundle #111 — live-session resilience, silent. Remaining: owner on-device verify of C1 -> Slice E flag default flip)

One-time, no rebuild. Combined with the code fix (x-mobile + always-set auth header) this should make the session persist.

items.title: Swap native JWT storage backend from UserDefaults to Keychain plugin
items.rationale: Final part of clerk-jwt-keychain. Replace @capacitor/preferences (UserDefaults, plaintext, backup-exposed) with @aparajita/capacitor-secure-storage using kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly so the Clerk JWT is encrypted at rest + excluded from device/iCloud backups. Thanks to native-token-store.ts this is a single-file backend swap (+ add the plugin + cap sync). NOTE: adds a native dependency (SPM/pod) and the actual Keychain persistence can only be fully verified on a real device/sim sign-in -> bundle with other device-verifiable changes or verify via a credentialed sim run before shipping. REQUIRED before App Store / wider release; NOT beta-blocking (owner-only TestFlight).
review_followups: LOW (TOCTOU, reviewer 2026-06-28): an in-flight FAPI response can re-persist the JWT just after clearNativeToken() on sign-out. Security-nil (Clerk destroys the session server-side; re-persisted token is revoked). Fix when doing the Keychain swap: gate setNativeToken on a module-level 'signed out' flag that clearNativeToken sets and a sign-in transition (false->true) resets — taking care NOT to suppress the re-sign-in token write (it happens during the sign_in FAPI exchange, before isSignedIn flips).

LOW (cold-start stale token, reviewer 2026-06-28): if sign-out happens while the app is killed, the stale (already server-invalid) JWT isn't cleared until it's injected and rejected. Fix: in nativeOnAfterResponse, clearNativeToken() on a FAPI authentication-failure error code — but first confirm the EXACT Clerk FAPI error code via a credentialed run so a valid token is never wrongly wiped.
items.defer_note: Auth/JWT storage swap + new native dependency (@aparajita/capacitor-secure-storage) + needs /security-review. Login breakage on real builds went uncaught before (see memory qa-test-critical-flows). Build in an ATTENDED session so login can be device-verified before ship — not unattended loop work.
items.why: Final part of clerk-jwt-keychain. Replace @capacitor/preferences (UserDefaults, plaintext, backup-exposed) with @aparajita/capacitor-secure-storage using kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly so the Clerk JWT is encrypted at rest + excluded from device/iCloud backups. Thanks to native-token-store.ts this is a single-file backend swap (+ add the plugin + cap sync). NOTE: adds a native dependency (SPM/pod) and the actual Keychain persistence can only be fully verified on a real device/sim sign-in -> bundle with other device-verifiable changes or verify via a credentialed sim run before shipping. REQUIRED before App Store / wider release; NOT beta-blocking (owner-only TestFlight).

items.title: EPIC: Map/GPS + caddie intelligence (plays-like, DECADE-style management)
items.rationale: Owner strategic direction (2026-06-28). Caddie that gives plays-like yardages + 'hit this club' using contours/slope + weather/wind, plus a DECADE-style expected-strokes course-management optimizer we build ourselves. Strategy: LICENSE the commodity layer (catalog, scorecard, F/C/B points, hazard/green polygons), BUILD the terrain intelligence (USGS 3DEP/NAIP, free) + the management engine + clean API; LICENSE-OR-SKIP green-reading (needs survey-grade data). KEY FINDING from planning: most caddie math ALREADY EXISTS (backend/app/caddie/strokes_gained.py, dispersion.py, club_selection.py plays-like, services/weather.py, services/elevation.py) — gaps are a true optimizer, a 3DEP precompute pipeline, tactical slope advice, and on-course UX. See phased cards tagged epic=map-gps-caddie-intelligence.

items.title: Feed licensed hazard/green polygons into hole intelligence
items.rationale: course_intel.build_hole_intelligence should consume hole_features polygons (water/bunker/ob) from PostGIS, preferring them over OSM; compute side+distance from geometry. Better inputs for everything downstream. Needs a polygon data source (license eval).
items.why: course_intel.build_hole_intelligence should consume hole_features polygons (water/bunker/ob) from PostGIS, preferring them over OSM; compute side+distance from geometry. Better inputs for everything downstream. Needs a polygon data source (license eval).

items.title: USGS 3DEP COG ingestion pipeline (per course)
items.rationale: Offline job: fetch 3DEP 1m bare-earth DEM for a course bbox, build a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF, upload to S3; read via rasterio/rio-tiler (windowed). Add rasterio/rio-tiler/boto3. Pilot on a handful of courses. First real heavy lift. Risks: CRS/data gaps/S3 cost.
items.why: Offline job: fetch 3DEP 1m bare-earth DEM for a course bbox, build a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF, upload to S3; read via rasterio/rio-tiler (windowed). Add rasterio/rio-tiler/boto3. Pilot on a handful of courses. First real heavy lift. Risks: CRS/data gaps/S3 cost.

items.title: Per-hole elevation profile precompute + store
items.rationale: Sample tee→green line + green grid from the COG into a hole_elevation_profiles table; replaces live USGS EPQS for profiled courses (EPQS stays fallback). Enables cheap plays-like lookups.
items.why: Sample tee→green line + green grid from the COG into a hole_elevation_profiles table; replaces live USGS EPQS for profiled courses (EPQS stays fallback). Enables cheap plays-like lookups.

items.title: Target-point plays-like (any aim point, not just green center)
items.rationale: Cheap elevation lookup for an arbitrary aim/target lat-lng from the profile/COG so plays-like is correct for layups and the optimizer's chosen target. Cache windowed reads for latency.
items.why: Cheap elevation lookup for an arbitrary aim/target lat-lng from the profile/COG so plays-like is correct for layups and the optimizer's chosen target. Cache windowed reads for latency.

items.title: Tap-to-target on the map → live plays-like + recommendation
items.rationale: Add aim-point selection in GPSMapView.tsx; on tap, compute bearing/distance and call recommend with the target so plays-like + optimizer target render on the map. Device-tested (map/GPS).
items.why: Add aim-point selection in GPSMapView.tsx; on tap, compute bearing/distance and call recommend with the target so plays-like + optimizer target render on the map. Device-tested (map/GPS).

items.title: Voice tactical advice (speak the plays-like number + 'club down')
items.rationale: Surface optimizer target + slope/miss lines through /session/voice context so the spoken caddie says the plays-like number and the tactic. Builds on the conversational caddie.
items.why: Surface optimizer target + slope/miss lines through /session/voice context so the spoken caddie says the plays-like number and the tactic. Builds on the conversational caddie.

items.title: Nationwide DEM/NAIP automation + optional ML feature extraction
items.rationale: Generalize the COG pipeline to many courses; optionally use NAIP to auto-extract fairway/green/hazard polygons where licensed feeds lack them. NEVER derive from Google/Mapbox imagery. Defer until pilots proven. Cost/ML-accuracy/ops risk.
items.why: Generalize the COG pipeline to many courses; optionally use NAIP to auto-extract fairway/green/hazard polygons where licensed feeds lack them. NEVER derive from Google/Mapbox imagery. Defer until pilots proven. Cost/ML-accuracy/ops risk.

items.title: On-device offline plays-like blob
items.rationale: Ship per-round elevation-profile JSON to the device (Capacitor Preferences) for offline lookup on-course. Device-tested.
items.why: Ship per-round elevation-profile JSON to the device (Capacitor Preferences) for offline lookup on-course. Device-tested.

items.title: Green-reading / putt-break (LICENSE or SKIP)
items.rationale: Integrate a survey-grade green-contour vendor (e.g. iGolf Green Heat Maps) for putt break. Do NOT derive from 1m DEM (physical resolution ceiling). Gated on an owner licensing decision; out of scope until decided.
items.why: Integrate a survey-grade green-contour vendor (e.g. iGolf Green Heat Maps) for putt break. Do NOT derive from 1m DEM (physical resolution ceiling). Gated on an owner licensing decision; out of scope until decided.

items.title: Per-player tee groups (data model + scoring) for 'Dan & I blues, Matt & John whites'
items.rationale: The Realtime set_round_setup tool captures per-player tees, but Round/Player carry a single teeName. To honor split tees end-to-end, add per-player tee to the Player/round model + UI + handicap/scoring implications, and map the tool's players[].tee through. Until then the agent uses a single default tee (first player's).

Until then the agent uses a single default tee (first player's).

items.title: Surface Playing Partners (unhide the orphaned /players roster)
items.rationale: The full SavedPlayer roster CRUD already exists at /players but is linked from nowhere. Rename/restyle to 'Playing Partners' (yardage-book), add two quiet entry points: home masthead/profile link + inline in the round-setup & tee-time player pickers. No backend. NO bottom tab bar (see ui_decision). First, lowest-risk slice of the social epic.

Real social requires relaxing this for new social routes (use current_user_id with per-row authz). This gate change is the security-review hotspot — MUST pass /security-review + /code-review. Gates A5/A6. DECISION NEEDED from owner: open the app beyond owner-only?

items.title: Phone-number friend matching (privacy-preserving)
items.rationale: Capacitor contacts plugin (net-new) + explicit consent screen + client-side E.164 normalize + HMAC-SHA256 hash (never upload raw numbers) → POST /api/partners/match against hashes of opted-in users. Per-user 'discoverable by phone' toggle (default off). NSContactsUsageDescription + Apple privacy manifest. Privacy/legal (TCPA) — highest-risk card.

items.title: Push-delivered invites (rare-by-design)
items.rationale: Capacitor push plugin + APNs; push ONLY for accepted/tee-time-found invite moments (strict trigger list — notifications are rare by design per CLAUDE.md). Device-token store + send. Do last.

Touches supabase/migrations (guard-protected — owner applies). Sequence LAST; B1–B4 deliberately avoid depending on it by keying reviews on golfApiCourseId.

items.title: Location-aware course-name disambiguation (Bally not Valley)
items.rationale: Owner 2026-06-28: resolve spoken/typed course names against real nearby courses using current location. BLOCKED on the course search+DB epic (needs the resolver + course DB foundation). Lands at epic iteration 3.
items.why: Owner 2026-06-28: resolve spoken/typed course names against real nearby courses using current location. BLOCKED on the course search+DB epic (needs the resolver + course DB foundation). Lands at epic iteration 3.

items.title: EPIC: Course search + homegrown course DB (no GolfAPI)
items.rationale: Owner pivot: build course data from NAIP+OSM+USGS 3DEP + own PostGIS vectors. See specs/homegrown-course-data-plan.md. POC = Bethpage Black (I0-I4); generalize later (I5-I10). Resolver/search layer + location-aware disambiguation ride on top.

items.title: Per-user voice language setting (onboarding + profile)
items.why: Owner 2026-07-06: caddie transcribed him in Korean (auto-detect). Transcription is now PINNED to English (realtime_relay.py) as the default; when onboarding exists, add a language picker (profile-stored, feeds the transcription language + instructions) instead of the hardcode.

items.title: Distinct persona TTS voices (each caddie persona has its own spoken voice)
items.why: Seeded 2026-07-07 (retro). Now that opt-in TTS sheet replies shipped (voice-tts-sheet-replies, #102) with ONE default voice, the natural next step toward the 'personal caddie' Northstar is mapping each persona to a distinct gpt-4o-mini-tts voice + delivery so 'Classic on the bag' and a brasher persona actually SOUND different. Small, additive to the existing /api/voice/speak proxy + useSheetTTS (persona already threaded). Keep it calm/voice-first; owner may want to pick/preview a voice per persona. Candidate — needs a short spec + owner nod on which personas/voices.

items.title: Cached per-hole caddie strategy guides (LLM writer grounded in our geometry + web)
items.why: RECONCILED 2026-07-10: code IS on main (guide_writer.py + course_guides.py, diff-vs-main empty). Two activation paths: (1) on-demand — caddie.py start_session line ~324 seeds guides for a cold course on first play (RUNS in prod if prod ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + web_search work); (2) run_guide_backfill env-gated, EMPTY allowlist default → dormant unless GUIDE_BACKFILL_COURSES set. BLOCKING GAP (owner's 'test-critical-flows' memory): the live research path (web_search_20260209 + messages.parse + pause_turn continuation) was NEVER smoke-tested against a live key — and it's best-effort, so a failure is SILENT (guides never appear, no error). NEEDS: a bounded prod live-smoke (one hole/one course via SSM on-box, ~small API spend — OWNER OK since it spends prod budget) to confirm guides actually generate + are grounded; if broken, fix. Security/code review of the web-content-into-LLM surface (plan §9) also owed. | OWNER UNPAUSED 2026-07-09: 'Maybe a tiny bit of context added there would help to understand the shot and hole better.' Confirmed today the caddie has NO researched course knowledge (only our geometry/hazards/weather/player context). PHASE 1 (build): an LLM writer researches each hole (web search + our stored geometry as GROUND TRUTH — the guide may never contradict real hazards/yardage; HAZARD_GR
items.resolution: PROD LIVE-SMOKE VERIFIED 2026-07-11 (owner OK'd): research_hole_guide→validate_guide runs end-to-end vs prod key (claude-sonnet-5 + web_search + messages.parse + pause_turn); 37 guides already persisted (Bethpage Black 18 + Pebble 19); fresh Bethpage-7 smoke returned a real grounded guide (cites real bunkers/trees at correct dists/sides), validate_guide PASSED. NOT silently broken — healthy. One low-pri follow-up: pause_turn continuation re-serialization warnings (see guide-pauseturn-reserialize-hardening).

items.title: Persist static course intel (elevation deltas + green slope) once per course
items.why: Owner ask (2026-07-08): elevation deltas + green slope are static per course. Compute once EVER per course, persist on the mapped course record (backend), serve instantly to course-intel. Kills the owner's 'elevation takes a while' wait. Backend-only (course record); no app-visible change beyond speed.
items.resolution: shipped-to-bundle (integration/next, PR #133). SILENT (backend-only; observable change = SPEED). Cycle 86 (2026-07-11). KEY FINDING on trace: ~90% was ALREADY BUILT in prior commits 0200576 + 9c5e338 — static per-hole intel (tee/green elevation, delta_ft, plays_like_yards, green_slope) is persisted in hole_features.properties JSONB, read instantly by build_hole_intelligence (ZERO USGS/3DEP on hit), bulk-precomputed (_precompute_course_elevations, 2 batched 3DEP calls), backfilled-on-demand, and honest (USGS-None persists nothing, no fabricated zeros). Weather stays DYNAMIC (correct). Fable plan v2 (specs/course-intel-static-persistence-plan.md) RULED against a new courses.static_intel column + Alembic migration: static intel is per-HOLE and already lives correctly per-feature; a course-level column would duplicate a 2nd source of truth (worse). NO migration. This cycle closed the two remaining gaps: (A) elevation precompute is now wired into create_mapped/put_mapped (was only /session/start → raced the first course-intel open on a fresh course), ordered BEFORE guides; (B) content-addressed invalidation — a new elevation_coords_key (tee+green centers @6dp) stamped into the JSONB by the precompute; the skip now requires an exact key match (was 'has tee_elevation_ft'), so a geometry re-map recomputes stale elevation. Also stamps elevation_computed_at (observability). Precompute + 2 helpers moved verbatim caddie.py → NEW app/services/course_elevation.py (import hygiene, no cycle). Request-path write-back stays KEYLESS (samples client coords it can't vouch for → treated as needs-revalidation). Read/hot path UNCHANGED. Commits: plan @d2d322d, feature @f682f85, head @efb32f4. Reviewer (fresh, adversarial): SHIP — static-only, invalidation deterministic (no lng/lat transposition, both sides from one _feature_center pipeline), write-back keyless, no read-path regression, no fabrication, no new concurrency hazard, tests faithfully encode spec (no weakened assertions), ruff clean. QA gates green on head: ruff clean, non-DB pytest 2060 passed/92 skipped, REAL docker Postgres (postgis:16-3.4) integration 92 passed incl mapped-course-db 10/10 (3 new precompute cases: idempotency-via-key, remap-invalidation round-trip, graceful-degrade), frontend lint/tsc clean + voice 277/277 (frontend untouched). Rides bundle #133 — no ship/ping (owner approves the whole bundle on next noticeable change).

items.title: Tee-time searching-transcript copy: drop the double reservation disclaimer + unify route/real-slot verb
items.notes: Designer (cycle 43) nits 2+3 on the S1 path (non-blocking): (2) looperLine 'they take the reservation, book it on the course site' + subCopy 'You book direct — the course takes the reservation' say the same thing back-to-back (reads SaaS-legal); shorten looperLine to the fact only. (3) route path says 'Pulling up how to book.' vs real-slot 'Setting it up.' — unify to one caddie verb. confirm-copy.ts:56, page.tsx:914-916. Item 1 (time format) already fixed in 3ce8bd5.
items.why: | SCOPED 2026-07-10: cosmetic copy on the tee-time 'searching…' state; no recorded defect, needs a concrete before/after from the owner or a designer pass to be worth a cycle. Low value solo.

items.title: A/B raise server_vad threshold 0.5→0.6-0.7 (distant voices fall below trigger)
items.why: Voice target-speaker spike (PR #127): the only remaining Tier-1 lever after the already-shipped mic constraints — raise server_vad energy threshold so distant partner voices don't trigger the caddie. Behavior-change A/B (too high = misses the owner's soft speech), so measure, don't ship blind. specs/voice-target-speaker-spike-report.md.

items.title: S4d: rung-2b headless scraper (Playwright worker) + 'checking…' pending UX
items.why: DEFERRED per Fable verdict (specs/teetime-headless-scraper-plan.md §7): NO bucket-(c) target in NY-metro — every remaining engine is httpx-addable (CPS/Quick18/Teesnap) or anti-bot-walled (EZLinks→rung3, browser can't pass Turnstile politely). ~400MB Chromium would fetch ZERO extra courses = headless-for-its-own-sake. Fully specced+gated; reopen ONLY if the coverage flywheel finds a JS-render-gated, non-anti-bot engine with no replayable XHR. | NOTE 2026-07-10: EZLinks/Cloudflare-Turnstile sites are NOT scrapable politely even with a headless browser (probe confirmed Chromium never clears Turnstile) — do NOT target them with S4d. S4d headless is only for JS-rendered widgets WITHOUT anti-bot gates. Re-scope S4d's target engine after S4a/S4c reveal which engines actually need a browser. The only slice with deploy-image impact (~400MB Chromium, gated TEETIME_SCRAPER_ENABLED default off). In-process asyncio worker + bounded queue off the request path; TeeTimeSlot gains status live|pending; poll endpoint; frontend pending row; first browser-scraped engine (Club Prophet or hostile EZLinks). Plan §4b,§6,§7. Depends on S4a.

items.title: S4f: coverage flywheel — auto-probe on honest-empty telemetry + coverage metric + drift canary
items.why: Bundle-rider. Log searched courses with no capability row → feed the probe script; '% of searched courses returning real availability' metric; monthly re-probe sweep; per-engine schema-drift canary. Plan §2b,§9.

items.title: [SHELVED] Voice-clone caller pipeline (Option A) — owner's actual voice via ElevenLabs split bridge
items.why: Owner chose Option B (preset) over this 2026-07-10. Full plan ON SHELF: specs/voice-clone-caller-plan.md §7 (V1 enrollment → V2 ElevenLabs cloned-TTS → V3 split bridge behind flag → V4 latency harness). ~3-4 cycles. Pull off the shelf only if owner later wants his ACTUAL voice for its own sake. Fable verdict: buildable + cheaper/call but adds latency + a vendor holding his voiceprint + buys nothing on a disclosed-AI call.

items.title: H3: Teesnap httpx adapter (conditional on XSRF/cookie capture)
items.why: Fable scraper plan §6/H3: Teesnap = Laravel/CloudFront (NOT Cloudflare challenge), React widget → customer JSON API, XSRF-TOKEN+laravel_session cookies. Discover a live NY facility subdomain, capture the availability JSON path, confirm cookie/XSRF replay over httpx → adapter+fixture. If a real bot-block appears → reclassify rung-3, no headless.

items.title: Harden guide_writer pause_turn continuation re-serialization (low-pri)
items.why: Prod smoke (2026-07-11) noted: guide_writer.py:278 re-serializes assistant content via model_dump(mode='json') in the pause_turn continuation loop; when the model returns code_execution/text_editor_code_execution server-tool blocks (not web_search), pydantic emits PydanticSerializationUnexpectedValue warnings. NON-FATAL (the smoke hole finished first-turn), but a hole needing a real pause_turn continuation with those block types may not re-serialize cleanly. Fix: resume with the SDK block objects directly rather than a manual model_dump. Low priority.

ui_decision_social_course: OWNER OVERRIDE (2026-06-28, IMG_2960): build a floating 'island' pill bottom tab bar (Instagram-style — rounded, hovers above the bottom edge, NOT edge-to-edge SaaS) for navigating the future features. This supersedes the earlier 'no bottom tab' recommendation. Build it in the yardage-book aesthetic (on-paper, restrained, calm — not generic SaaS) and HIDE it on immersive screens (active round / voice / GPS) where the app should disappear. Likely tabs: Round/Home · Partners · Courses (+ profile). This is the 'minimal home dock' path from the plan, now promoted to the chosen approach + extended to a persistent floating tab. A new card 'nav-floating-island-tab' tracks the build.
